data.description: For most games and apps made with 4.4 it should be relatively safe to migrate to 4.5. This page intends to cover everything you need to pay attention to when migrating your project. Breaking change...

  --fa-font-thumbprint-light: normal 300 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Thumbprint";
  --fa-font-notdog-solid: normal 900 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Notdog";
  --fa-font-notdog-duo-solid: normal 900 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Notdog Duo";
  --fa-font-etch-solid: normal 900 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Etch";
  --fa-font-jelly-regular: normal 400 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Jelly";
  --fa-font-jelly-fill-regular: normal 400 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Jelly Fill";
  --fa-font-jelly-duo-regular: normal 400 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Jelly Duo";
  --fa-font-chisel-regular: normal 400 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Chisel";
  --fa-font-utility-semibold: normal 600 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Utility";
  --fa-font-utility-duo-semibold: normal 600 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Utility Duo";
  --fa-font-utility-fill-semibold: normal 600 1em/1 "Font Awesome 7 Utility Fill";
}

.svg-inline--fa {
  box-sizing: content-box;
  display: var(--fa-display, inline-block);
  height: 1em;
  overflow: visible;
  vertical-align: -0.125em;
  width: var(--fa-width, 1.25em);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-2xs {
  vertical-align: 0.1em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-xs {
  vertical-align: 0em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-sm {
  vertical-align: -0.0714285714em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-lg {
  vertical-align: -0.2em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-xl {
  vertical-align: -0.25em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-2xl {
  vertical-align: -0.3125em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-pull-left,
.svg-inline--fa .fa-pull-start {
  float: inline-start;
  margin-inline-end: var(--fa-pull-margin, 0.3em);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-pull-right,
.svg-inline--fa .fa-pull-end {
  float: inline-end;
  margin-inline-start: var(--fa-pull-margin, 0.3em);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-li {
  width: var(--fa-li-width, 2em);
  inset-inline-start: calc(-1 * var(--fa-li-width, 2em));
  inset-block-start: 0.25em; /* syncing vertical alignment with Web Font rendering */
}

.fa-layers-counter, .fa-layers-text {
  display: inline-block;
  position: absolute;
  text-align: center;
}

.fa-layers {
  display: inline-block;
  height: 1em;
  position: relative;
  text-align: center;
  vertical-align: -0.125em;
  width: var(--fa-width, 1.25em);
}
.fa-layers .svg-inline--fa {
  inset: 0;
  margin: auto;
  position: absolute;
  transform-origin: center center;
}

.fa-layers-text {
  left: 50%;
  top: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  transform-origin: center center;
}

.fa-layers-counter {
  background-color: var(--fa-counter-background-color, #ff253a);
  border-radius: var(--fa-counter-border-radius, 1em);
  box-sizing: border-box;
  color: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
  line-height: var(--fa-counter-line-height, 1);
  max-width: var(--fa-counter-max-width, 5em);
  min-width: var(--fa-counter-min-width, 1.5em);
  overflow: hidden;
  padding: var(--fa-counter-padding, 0.25em 0.5em);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-counter-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top right;
}

.fa-layers-bottom-right {
  bottom: var(--fa-bottom, 0);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  top: auto;
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: bottom right;
}

.fa-layers-bottom-left {
  bottom: var(--fa-bottom, 0);
  left: var(--fa-left, 0);
  right: auto;
  top: auto;
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: bottom left;
}

.fa-layers-top-right {
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top right;
}

.fa-layers-top-left {
  left: var(--fa-left, 0);
  right: auto;
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top left;
}

.fa-1x {
  font-size: 1em;
}

.fa-2x {
  font-size: 2em;
}

.fa-3x {
  font-size: 3em;
}

.fa-4x {
  font-size: 4em;
}

.fa-5x {
  font-size: 5em;
}

.fa-6x {
  font-size: 6em;
}

.fa-7x {
  font-size: 7em;
}

.fa-8x {
  font-size: 8em;
}

.fa-9x {
  font-size: 9em;
}

.fa-10x {
  font-size: 10em;
}

.fa-2xs {
  font-size: calc(10 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 10px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 10 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 10 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-xs {
  font-size: calc(12 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 12px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 12 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 12 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-sm {
  font-size: calc(14 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 14px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 14 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 14 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-lg {
  font-size: calc(20 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 20px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 20 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 20 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-xl {
  font-size: calc(24 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 24px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 24 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 24 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-2xl {
  font-size: calc(32 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 32px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 32 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 32 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-width-auto {
  --fa-width: auto;
}

.fa-fw,
.fa-width-fixed {
  --fa-width: 1.25em;
}

.fa-ul {
  list-style-type: none;
  margin-inline-start: var(--fa-li-margin, 2.5em);
  padding-inline-start: 0;
}
.fa-ul > li {
  position: relative;
}

.fa-li {
  inset-inline-start: calc(-1 * var(--fa-li-width, 2em));
  position: absolute;
  text-align: center;
  width: var(--fa-li-width, 2em);
  line-height: inherit;
}

/* Heads Up: Bordered Icons will not be supported in the future!
  - This feature will be deprecated in the next major release of Font Awesome (v8)!
  - You may continue to use it in this version *v7), but it will not be supported in Font Awesome v8.
*/
/* Notes:
* --@{v.$css-prefix}-border-width = 1/16 by default (to render as ~1px based on a 16px default font-size)
* --@{v.$css-prefix}-border-padding =
  ** 3/16 for vertical padding (to give ~2px of vertical whitespace around an icon considering it's vertical alignment)
  ** 4/16 for horizontal padding (to give ~4px of horizontal whitespace around an icon)
*/
.fa-border {
  border-color: var(--fa-border-color, #eee);
  border-radius: var(--fa-border-radius, 0.1em);
  border-style: var(--fa-border-style, solid);
  border-width: var(--fa-border-width, 0.0625em);
  box-sizing: var(--fa-border-box-sizing, content-box);
  padding: var(--fa-border-padding, 0.1875em 0.25em);
}

.fa-pull-left,
.fa-pull-start {
  float: inline-start;
  margin-inline-end: var(--fa-pull-margin, 0.3em);
}

.fa-pull-right,
.fa-pull-end {
  float: inline-end;
  margin-inline-start: var(--fa-pull-margin, 0.3em);
}

.fa-beat {
  animation-name: fa-beat;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, ease-in-out);
}

.fa-bounce {
  animation-name: fa-bounce;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.28, 0.84, 0.42, 1));
}

.fa-fade {
  animation-name: fa-fade;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1));
}

.fa-beat-fade {
  animation-name: fa-beat-fade;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1));
}

.fa-flip {
  animation-name: fa-flip;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, ease-in-out);
}

.fa-shake {
  animation-name: fa-shake;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, linear);
}

.fa-spin {
  animation-name: fa-spin;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 2s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, linear);
}

.fa-spin-reverse {
  --fa-animation-direction: reverse;
}

.fa-pulse,
.fa-spin-pulse {
  animation-name: fa-spin;
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, steps(8));
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .fa-beat,
  .fa-bounce,
  .fa-fade,
  .fa-beat-fade,
  .fa-flip,
  .fa-pulse,
  .fa-shake,
  .fa-spin,
  .fa-spin-pulse {
    animation: none !important;
    transition: none !important;
  }
}
@keyframes fa-beat {
  0%, 90% {
    transform: scale(1);
  }
  45% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-beat-scale, 1.25));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-bounce {
  0% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
  10% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-start-scale-x, 1.1), var(--fa-bounce-start-scale-y, 0.9)) translateY(0);
  }
  30% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-jump-scale-x, 0.9), var(--fa-bounce-jump-scale-y, 1.1)) translateY(var(--fa-bounce-height, -0.5em));
  }
  50% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-land-scale-x, 1.05), var(--fa-bounce-land-scale-y, 0.95)) translateY(0);
  }
  57% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(var(--fa-bounce-rebound, -0.125em));
  }
  64% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
  100% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-fade {
  50% {
    opacity: var(--fa-fade-opacity, 0.4);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-beat-fade {
  0%, 100% {
    opacity: var(--fa-beat-fade-opacity, 0.4);
    transform: scale(1);
  }
  50% {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: scale(var(--fa-beat-fade-scale, 1.125));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-flip {
  50% {
    transform: rotate3d(var(--fa-flip-x, 0), var(--fa-flip-y, 1), var(--fa-flip-z, 0), var(--fa-flip-angle, -180deg));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-shake {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(-15deg);
  }
  4% {
    transform: rotate(15deg);
  }
  8%, 24% {
    transform: rotate(-18deg);
  }
  12%, 28% {
    transform: rotate(18deg);
  }
  16% {
    transform: rotate(-22deg);
  }
  20% {
    transform: rotate(22deg);
  }
  32% {
    transform: rotate(-12deg);
  }
  36% {
    transform: rotate(12deg);
  }
  40%, 100% {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-spin {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
  100% {
    transform: rotate(360deg);
  }
}
.fa-rotate-90 {
  transform: rotate(90deg);
}

.fa-rotate-180 {
  transform: rotate(180deg);
}

.fa-rotate-270 {
  transform: rotate(270deg);
}

.fa-flip-horizontal {
  transform: scale(-1, 1);
}

.fa-flip-vertical {
  transform: scale(1, -1);
}

.fa-flip-both,
.fa-flip-horizontal.fa-flip-vertical {
  transform: scale(-1, -1);
}

.fa-rotate-by {
  transform: rotate(var(--fa-rotate-angle, 0));
}

.svg-inline--fa .fa-primary {
  fill: var(--fa-primary-color, currentColor);
  opacity: var(--fa-primary-opacity, 1);
}

.svg-inline--fa .fa-secondary {
  fill: var(--fa-secondary-color, currentColor);
  opacity: var(--fa-secondary-opacity, 0.4);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-swap-opacity .fa-primary {
  opacity: var(--fa-secondary-opacity, 0.4);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-swap-opacity .fa-secondary {
  opacity: var(--fa-primary-opacity, 1);
}

.svg-inline--fa mask .fa-primary,
.svg-inline--fa mask .fa-secondary {
  fill: black;
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-inverse {
  fill: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
}

.fa-stack {
  display: inline-block;
  height: 2em;
  line-height: 2em;
  position: relative;
  vertical-align: middle;
  width: 2.5em;
}

.fa-inverse {
  color: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-stack-1x {
  --fa-width: 1.25em;
  height: 1em;
  width: var(--fa-width);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-stack-2x {
  --fa-width: 2.5em;
  height: 2em;
  width: var(--fa-width);
}

.fa-stack-1x,
.fa-stack-2x {
  inset: 0;
  margin: auto;
  position: absolute;
  z-index: var(--fa-stack-z-index, auto);

  <section id="upgrading-from-godot-4-4-to-godot-4-5">
<span id="doc-upgrading-to-godot-4-5"></span><h1>Upgrading from Godot 4.4 to Godot 4.5<a class="headerlink" href="#upgrading-from-godot-4-4-to-godot-4-5" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>For most games and apps made with 4.4 it should be relatively safe to migrate to 4.5.
This page intends to cover everything you need to pay attention to when migrating
your project.</p>
<section id="breaking-changes">
<h2>Breaking changes<a class="headerlink" href="#breaking-changes" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>If you are migrating from 4.4 to 4.5, the breaking changes listed here might
affect you. Changes are grouped by areas/systems.</p>
<div class="admonition warning">
<p class="admonition-title">Warning</p>
<p>In order to support <a class="reference external" href="https://android-developers.googleblog.com/2025/05/prepare-play-apps-for-devices-with-16kb-page-size.html">new Google Play requirements</a> Android now requires
targeting .NET 9 when exporting C# projects to Android, other platforms
continue to use .NET 8 as the minimum required version but newer versions
are supported and encouraged.</p>
<p>If you are using C# in your project and want to export to Android, you will
need to upgrade your project to .NET 9 (see <a class="reference external" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/install/upgrade">Upgrading to a new .NET version</a>
for instructions).</p>
</div>
<p>This article indicates whether each breaking change affects GDScript and whether
the C# breaking change is <em>binary compatible</em> or <em>source compatible</em>:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>Binary compatible</strong> - Existing binaries will load and execute successfully without
recompilation, and the run-time behavior won't change.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>Source compatible</strong> - Source code will compile successfully without changes when
upgrading Godot.</p></li>
</ul>
<section id="core">
<h3>Core<a class="headerlink" href="#core" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<div class="wy-table-responsive"><table class="docutils align-default">
<thead>
<tr class="row-odd"><th class="head"><p>Change</p></th>
<th class="head"><p>GDScript Compatible</p></th>
<th class="head"><p>C# Binary Compatible</p></th>
<th class="head"><p>C# Source Compatible</p></th>
<th class="head"><p>Introduced</p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr class="row-even"><td><p><strong>JSONRPC</strong></p></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-odd"><td><p>Method <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">set_scope</span></code> replaced by <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">set_method</span></code></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API breaks compatibility.">❌</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="Stub compatibility methods were added to prevent crashes. However, this API is not functional anymore.">❌</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="Stub compatibility methods were added to prevent crashes. However, this API is not functional anymore.">❌</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104890">GH-104890</a></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-even"><td><p><strong>Node</strong></p></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-odd"><td><p>Method <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">get_rpc_config</span></code> renamed to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">get_node_rpc_config</span></code></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API breaks compatibility.">❌</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106848">GH-106848</a></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-even"><td><p>Method <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">set_name</span></code> changes <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">name</span></code> parameter type from <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">String</span></code> to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">StringName</span></code></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/76560">GH-76560</a></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></div>
</section>
<section id="rendering">
<h3>Rendering<a class="headerlink" href="#rendering" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<div class="wy-table-responsive"><table class="docutils align-default">
<thead>
<tr class="row-odd"><th class="head"><p>Change</p></th>

<th class="head"><p>C# Binary Compatible</p></th>
<th class="head"><p>C# Source Compatible</p></th>
<th class="head"><p>Introduced</p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr class="row-even"><td><p><strong>DisplayServer</strong></p></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-odd"><td><p>Method <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">file_dialog_show</span></code> adds a new <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">parent_window_id</span></code> optional parameter</p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/98194">GH-98194</a></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-even"><td><p>Method <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">file_dialog_with_options_show</span></code> adds a new <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">parent_window_id</span></code> optional parameter</p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/98194">GH-98194</a></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-odd"><td><p><strong>RenderingDevice</strong></p></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-even"><td><p>Method <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">texture_create_from_extension</span></code> adds a new <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">mipmaps</span></code> optional parameter</p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105570">GH-105570</a></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-odd"><td><p><strong>RenderingServer</strong></p></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-even"><td><p>Method <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">instance_reset_physics_interpolation</span></code> removed</p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API breaks compatibility.">❌</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104269">GH-104269</a></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="row-odd"><td><p>Method <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">instance_set_interpolated</span></code> removed</p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API breaks compatibility.">❌</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><abbr title="This API does not break compatibility. A compatibility method was added.">✔️</abbr></p></td>
<td><p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104269">GH-104269</a></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></div>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>In C#, the enum <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">RenderingDevice.Features</span></code> breaks compatibility because of the way the bindings generator
detects the enum prefix. New members were added to the enum in <a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/103941">GH-103941</a> that caused the enum member
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">Address</span></code> to be renamed to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">BufferDeviceAddress</span></code>.</p>
</div>
</section>

are only available in the editor. Using them outside of the editor will result in a compilation error.</p>
<p>In C#, this means the types are moved from the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">GodotSharp</span></code> assembly to the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">GodotSharpEditor</span></code> assembly.
Make sure to wrap code that uses these types in a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">#if</span> <span class="pre">TOOLS</span></code> block to ensure they are not included in an exported game.</p>
<p><strong>This change was also backported to 4.4.1.</strong></p>
</div>

<section id="behavior-changes">
<h2>Behavior changes<a class="headerlink" href="#behavior-changes" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>In 4.5, some behavior changes have been introduced, which might require you to adjust your project.</p>
<section id="tilemaplayer">
<h3>TileMapLayer<a class="headerlink" href="#tilemaplayer" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<p><a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_tilemaplayer.html#class-tilemaplayer-method-get-coords-for-body-rid"><span class="std std-ref">TileMapLayer.get_coords_for_body_rid()</span></a>
will return different values in 4.5 compared to 4.4,
as TileMapLayer physics chunking is enabled by default. Higher values of
<a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_tilemaplayer.html#class-tilemaplayer-property-physics-quadrant-size"><span class="std std-ref">TileMapLayer.physics_quadrant_size</span></a>
will make this function less precise. To get the exact cell coordinates like in 4.4 and prior
versions, you need to set
<a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_tilemaplayer.html#class-tilemaplayer-property-physics-quadrant-size"><span class="std std-ref">TileMapLayer.physics_quadrant_size</span></a>
to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">1</span></code>, which disables physics chunking.</p>
</section>
<section id="d-model-import">
<h3>3D Model Import<a class="headerlink" href="#d-model-import" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<p>A fix has been made to the 3D model importers to correctly handle non-joint nodes within a skeleton hierarchy (<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104184">GH-104184</a>).
To preserve compatibility, the default behavior is to import existing files with the same behavior as before (<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107352">GH-107352</a>).
New <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">.gltf</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">.glb</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">.blend</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">.fbx</span></code> files (without a corresponding <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">.import</span></code> file)
will be imported with the new behavior. However, for existing files, if you want to use the
new behavior, you must change the "Naming Version" option at the bottom of the Import dock:</p>
<img alt="../../_images/gltf_naming_version.webp" src="../../_images/gltf_naming_version.webp">
</section>
<section id="id1">
<h3>Core<a class="headerlink" href="#id1" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p><a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_resource.html#class-resource-method-duplicate"><span class="std std-ref">Resource.duplicate(true)</span></a> (which performs
deep duplication) now only duplicates resources internal to the resource file
it's called on. In 4.4, this duplicated everything instead, including external resources.
If you were deep-duplicating a resource that contained references to other
external resources, those external resources aren't duplicated anymore. You must call
<a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_resource.html#class-resource-method-duplicate-deep"><span class="std std-ref">Resource.duplicate_deep(DEEP_DUPLICATE_ALL)</span></a>
instead to keep the old behavior.</p>
</div>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p><a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_projectsettings.html#class-projectsettings-method-add-property-info"><span class="std std-ref">ProjectSettings.add_property_info()</span></a>
now prints a warning when the dictionary parameter has missing keys or invalid keys.
Most importantly, it will now warn when a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">usage</span></code> key is passed, as this key is not used.
This was also the case before 4.5, but it was silently ignored instead.
As a reminder, to set property usage information correctly, you must use
<a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_projectsettings.html#class-projectsettings-method-set-as-basic"><span class="std std-ref">ProjectSettings.set_as_basic()</span></a>,
<a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_projectsettings.html#class-projectsettings-method-set-restart-if-changed"><span class="std std-ref">ProjectSettings.set_restart_if_changed()</span></a>,
or <a class="reference internal" href="../../classes/class_projectsettings.html#class-projectsettings-method-set-as-internal"><span class="std std-ref">ProjectSettings.set_as_internal()</span></a> instead.</p>
</div>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>In C#, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">StringExtensions.PathJoin</span></code> now avoids adding an extra path separator when the original string is empty,
or when the appended path starts with a path separator (<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105281">GH-105281</a>).</p>
</div>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>In C#, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">StringExtensions.GetExtension</span></code> now returns an empty string instead of the original string
when the original string does not contain an extension (<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108041">GH-108041</a>).</p>
</div>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>In C#, the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">Quaternion(Vector3,</span> <span class="pre">Vector3)</span></code> constructor now correctly creates a quaternion representing
the shortest arc between the two input vectors. Previously, it would return incorrect values for certain inputs
(<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107618">GH-107618</a>).</p>
</div>
</section>
<section id="navigation">
<h3>Navigation<a class="headerlink" href="#navigation" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>By default, the regions in a NavigationServer map now update asynchronously using threads to improve performance.
This can cause additional delay in the update due to thread synchronisation.
The asynchronous region update can be toggled with the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">navigation/world/region_use_async_iterations</span></code> project setting.</p>
</div>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>The merging of navmeshes in the NavigationServer has changed processing order. Regions now merge and cache
internal navmeshes first, then the remaining free edges are merged by the navigation map.
If a project had navigation map synchronisation errors before, it might now have shifted
affected edges, making already existing errors in a layout more noticeable in the pathfinding.
The <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">navigation/2d_or_3d/merge_rasterizer_cell_scale</span></code> project setting can be set to a lower value
to increase the detail of the rasterization grid (with <cite>0.01</cite> being the smallest cell size possible).
If edge merge errors still persist with the lowest possible rasterization scale value,
the error may be caused by overlap: two navmeshes are stacked on top of each other, causing geometry conflict.</p>
</div>
</section>
<section id="physics">
<h3>Physics<a class="headerlink" href="#physics" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>When the 3D physics engine is set to Jolt Physics, you will now always have overlaps between <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">Area3D</span></code> and static
bodies reported by default, as the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">physics/jolt_physics_3d/simulation/areas_detect_static_bodies</span></code> project setting
has been removed (<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105746">GH-105746</a>). If you still want such overlaps to be ignored, you will need to change the collision mask
or layer of either the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">Area3D</span></code> or the static body instead.</p>
</div>
</section>
<section id="id2">
<h3>Text<a class="headerlink" href="#id2" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>In GDScript, calls to functions <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">RichTextLabel::add_image</span></code> and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">RichTextLabel::update_image</span></code> will continue to work,
but the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">size_in_percent</span></code> argument will now be used as the value for <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">width_in_percent</span></code> and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">height_in_percent</span></code>
will default to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">false</span></code> (<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107347">GH-107347</a>). To restore the previous behavior, you can explicitly set <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">height_in_percent</span></code>
to the same value you were passing as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">size_in_percent</span></code>.</p>
</div>
</section>
</section>
</section>

metadata.description: For most games and apps made with 4.4 it should be relatively safe to migrate to 4.5. This page intends to cover everything you need to pay attention to when migrating your project. Breaking change...
